bifocal.target: Query-Dependent Source Credibility — dynamic credibility weighting by query intent classification

spec_corrections.note: Feature spec contains 3 factual errors about the existing codebase

corrections.claim: source_credibility is one of the scoring dimensions (0.10 weight) in ProactiveRelevanceEngine
corrections.actual: FALSE. RelevanceEngine (src/relevance.rs) has 7 dimensions in LearnedWeights: semantic, entity, tag, importance, momentum, access_count, graph_strength. There is NO source_credibility dimension. Credibility is used as a tiny additive boost in 3 separate retrieval paths (recall.rs:1952, graph_retrieval.rs:1000, retrieval.rs:2049) but NOT as a weight in the fused scoring.

corrections.claim: semantic_retrieve Layer 5 unified scoring includes source credibility as static factor
corrections.actual: FALSE. No function called semantic_retrieve exists. Retrieval goes through RelevanceEngine.surface_relevant() (relevance.rs:711), MemorySystem.recall_with_feedback() (recall.rs), and graph_retrieval::retrieve_with_spreading_activation(). Credibility is a +0.05 additive in retrieval.rs:2049 (only when >0.8) and (credibility-0.5)*0.1 in recall.rs:1952.
PARTIALLY WRONG. The enum variant is ExternalApi, not Api. parse_source_type() maps both 'api' and 'external_api' strings to ExternalApi.

query_intent_classifier.reason: New code path on critical retrieval hot path. Must be <1ms. String pattern matching, no external deps, no state. Medium risk.

weight_matrix_lookup.reason: Pure function, static const arrays. Zero risk.

8th_dimension_in_learned_weights.reason: CRITICAL. Changes weight normalization for ALL existing users. fuse_scores_full() is called in 4 code paths (relevance.rs:847, relevance.rs:1048, and 2 more via learned weights). Adding a dimension redistributes weight budget. Breaks backward compat of serialized LearnedWeights.

source_type_data_quality.reason: CRITICAL. hooks/memory-hook.ts does NOT set source_type. parse_source_type() defaults to SourceType::User when None is provided (remember.rs:217). This means ALL hook-ingested memories are labeled User — the HIGHEST credibility source type for FACTUAL queries. The feature would boost every hook memory for factual queries, defeating the purpose.

credibility_in_existing_retrieval_paths.reason: Credibility already applied in 3 retrieval paths as additive boost. The proposed change would add a 4th path (fused scoring) while the existing 3 remain. Potential double-counting.

failure_modes: AMBIGUOUS QUERIES: 'How did Bob fix the auth bug?' is both FACTUAL (who=Bob, what=auth bug) and PATTERN (how=methodology). The 3-class taxonomy is insufficient for compound queries.
MULTILINGUAL: Pattern matching on 'who/what/where/when' fails for non-English queries. shodh-memory is used internationally via hooks.
FALSE FACTUAL: 'What patterns do you see in our codebase?' starts with 'what' but is a PATTERN query. 'Who tends to work on frontend?' starts with 'who' but asks for PATTERN.
EXPLORATORY OVERFIT: 'What do I know about React hooks?' and 'Tell me everything about the auth system' are both EXPLORATORY but have very different ideal source weighting.
edge.contracts: Must return in <1ms. No heap allocation. No external calls.
edge.attack_surface: None — input is already-validated query string

fractal.pattern: Query classification -> weight modulation is a standard IR technique (query-dependent scoring)
fractal.mirror_test: BREAKS at micro scale: the 3-class taxonomy doesn't match the continuous nature of query intent. Real queries exist on a spectrum. The hard boundaries (FACTUAL vs PATTERN) create cliff effects where similar queries get wildly different rankings.
fractal.siblings: Existing query analysis: wants_recent detection (recall.rs:1921-1926) uses keyword matching. has_temporal detection uses temporal_refs. Both are binary (boost or no boost), not a 3-class taxonomy. This would be the first multi-class query classifier in the codebase.

hazop.NO_classification: Default to EXPLORATORY (all-equal). Graceful degradation but feature becomes inert.
hazop.MORE_classes: Adding 4th+ classes (PROCEDURAL, COMPARATIVE, CAUSAL) requires N*M weight matrix growth. Maintenance burden scales quadratically.
hazop.LESS_signal: When source_type is Unknown (most memories), the weight matrix maps to a fallback. With Unknown handling, feature is mostly inactive.
hazop.REVERSE_polarity: Misclassifying a FACTUAL query as PATTERN would DEMOTE user-stated facts and BOOST inferred content. This is actively harmful — worse than no feature.
hazop.PART_OF_query: Compound queries ('Who suggested X and why did it work?') need decomposition before classification. Without decomposition, the classifier sees the whole string and must pick one class.
hazop.OTHER_THAN_keywords: Could use embedding-space classification instead of keyword matching. More accurate but violates <1ms constraint.

fmea.failure_mode: Misclassification of FACTUAL as PATTERN demotes correct answers
fmea.mitigation: Add confidence score to classification. Only apply source weighting when confidence > 0.8, else fall back to EXPLORATORY.

failure_modes: CRITICAL: Weight redistribution. Current 7 weights sum to 1.0. Adding an 8th with default weight (e.g., 0.10) means all other weights lose ~1.4% each after normalization. This changes ranking for EVERY query, not just ones where source credibility matters.

CRITICAL: Serialization backward compat. LearnedWeights is serialized to RocksDB. Existing stored weights have 7 fields. Deserializing with serde(default) adds the 8th at default value, but the sum is now >1.0 until normalize() is called. If normalize() is not called on load, weights are wrong.

Feedback learning: apply_feedback() updates weights based on which components contributed. The new dimension needs feedback attribution — 'was source credibility the reason this memory was surfaced?' This is impossible to attribute cleanly because it's entangled with importance and entity scores.
edge.contracts: Must maintain: sum(weights) == 1.0 invariant. Must not increase fuse_scores_full() latency measurably.

fractal.pattern: Adding a dimension to a weighted linear combination
fractal.mirror_test: HOLDS at structure level (same pattern as adding graph_strength in CTX-3). BREAKS at semantic level: graph_strength is a memory-intrinsic property (how connected is this memory?). Source credibility is query-dependent (same memory gets different credibility for different queries). This is a fundamentally different kind of signal — it should be a score MODIFIER, not a weight DIMENSION.
fractal.key_insight: The existing 7 dimensions are all query-independent or memory-intrinsic. Source credibility weighting is the first query-dependent dimension. This is architecturally novel and doesn't fit the current fuse_scores_full() contract.

hazop.NO_weight: Set weight to 0.0. Feature is inert. Same as not implementing.
hazop.MORE_weight: If learned feedback amplifies source_credibility weight to 0.3+, it drowns semantic similarity. A low-credibility but perfectly relevant memory ranks below a high-credibility irrelevant one.
hazop.LESS_weight: At 0.02-0.05, the signal is too weak to affect ranking in the typical fused score range of 0.4-0.8.
hazop.REVERSE: Bug in weight matrix (transposed FACTUAL/PATTERN rows) silently inverts the feature. No detection mechanism.
hazop.EARLY_feedback: First few feedback signals could push credibility weight to extreme values via the learning rate. MIN_WEIGHT (0.02) prevents zeroing but not over-amplification.

fmea.failure_mode: Weight redistribution degrades existing retrieval quality for all queries
fmea.mitigation: Do NOT add as 8th dimension. Instead, apply as a post-fusion score multiplier: final_score *= effective_credibility. This avoids weight redistribution entirely.

failure_modes: FATAL: hooks/memory-hook.ts does NOT pass source_type to the API. The remember handler's parse_source_type() returns SourceType::User when input is None (remember.rs:217). This means ALL automatically-ingested memories (the vast majority) are labeled User — the highest-credibility source for FACTUAL queries.

FATAL: Even memories created via the MCP server's remember tool only set source_type if the calling LLM explicitly provides it. In practice, Claude/Cursor rarely set source_type — they pass content, tags, and maybe emotion.

Data: In a typical shodh-memory database, estimated >95% of memories have source_type=User (via None->default) and credibility=0.7 (via default_credibility). The weight matrix would apply User weights to nearly everything, making the feature a no-op scaled credibility boost.
edge.contracts: Feature requires accurate source_type metadata to function. Current data pipeline does not produce this.
edge.attack_surface: None, but data quality issue means feature is inert

fractal.pattern: Feature depends on metadata quality. Metadata is not reliably produced.
fractal.mirror_test: EXACT MATCH with a known anti-pattern: building a sophisticated ranking signal on unreliable input data. The emotional_valence/arousal fields have the same problem — they're defined but rarely populated, so the arousal boost is almost always 0.0.
fractal.prior_art: This is the same failure mode as the entity overlap boost before stemming: the signal exists in theory but the data pipeline doesn't produce reliable inputs.

hazop.NO_source_type: 95%+ of memories default to User. Feature is a no-op for most data.
hazop.OTHER_THAN_user: Memories explicitly created via MCP remember with source_type set ARE the minority that would benefit. But these are also the memories most likely to already have high credibility (0.8+).
hazop.PART_OF_pipeline: Only the remember handler sets source_type. The hooks, bulk import, and migration paths don't.

fmea.failure_mode: Feature is inert due to missing source_type metadata
fmea.note: Low severity because inert means no harm, but wasted effort

edge.data_crossing: credibility is already used as additive boost in 3 paths: recall.rs:1952 ((cred-0.5)*0.1), graph_retrieval.rs:1000 ((cred-0.5)*0.1), retrieval.rs:2049 (+0.05 when >0.8). Adding it to fused scoring creates 4th usage.
failure_modes: Double counting: A memory with credibility 0.9 gets +0.04 from recall.rs:1952 AND the fused dimension boost. The additive and multiplicative paths compound.
Inconsistent paths: The 3 existing paths use DIFFERENT formulas for credibility boost. Adding a 4th formula increases inconsistency.

fractal.pattern: Signal applied in multiple stages of the same pipeline
fractal.mirror_test: BREAKS. The recency signal is also applied in multiple paths but is CONSISTENT (always exponential decay). Credibility would be inconsistent: additive in some paths, multiplicative (via weight) in the fused path.

phase_2_fractal_atam.core_pattern: Query-dependent score modulation: classify query -> lookup weights -> modulate per-memory signal
phase_2_fractal_atam.macro_consistency: HOLDS — query-dependent behavior exists (wants_recent, has_temporal). Pattern is established.
phase_2_fractal_atam.meso_consistency: BREAKS — existing query-dependent signals are binary boosts (present/absent), not multi-class weight matrices. This is a higher-complexity pattern than any existing query-dependent signal.
phase_2_fractal_atam.micro_consistency: BREAKS — the weight matrix is a static lookup table, but the rest of the scoring system uses learned weights that adapt via feedback. Static vs learned creates a maintainability mismatch.

atam_tradeoffs.sensitivity: Feature could improve precision by 1-3% IF source_type data is accurate AND query classification is correct. Both conditions are currently unmet.
atam_tradeoffs.tradeoff: Adding complexity to scoring pipeline for a signal that is currently inert due to data quality issues.

atam_tradeoffs.sensitivity: 8th dimension in LearnedWeights increases API surface, test matrix, and serialization complexity.
atam_tradeoffs.tradeoff: Every future scoring change must account for the credibility dimension. Weight normalization, feedback attribution, A/B testing all gain a dimension.

atam_tradeoffs.sensitivity: Query classification adds <1ms. fuse_scores_full() gains one multiply-add (<1ns). Negligible.
HIGH. Misclassification actively harms ranking. Default source_type=User makes the feature boost all memories equally for FACTUAL queries, providing no discrimination.
atam_tradeoffs.tradeoff: Feature that cannot distinguish sources provides no value over uniform weighting.

phase_2_5_adversarial_challenge.note: No scores >= 7.0 to challenge. All dimensions scored conservatively due to data quality blocker.

adversarial_scenarios.scenario: User stores 100 memories via hooks (all source_type=User by default), then asks 'What patterns have I noticed about React performance?'
adversarial_scenarios.expected_behavior: PATTERN query should boost Inferred/AiGenerated and demote User sources
adversarial_scenarios.actual_behavior: ALL 100 memories are source_type=User. Weight matrix applies User=0.6 to all. No discrimination. Feature is a uniform 0.6 multiplier — same as not having the feature.
adversarial_scenarios.impact: Feature provides zero value for the primary use case (hook-ingested memories)

adversarial_scenarios.scenario: User explicitly sets source_type on 10 MCP memories (mix of User, Inferred, AiGenerated). Asks 'Who is the project lead?'
adversarial_scenarios.expected_behavior: FACTUAL query. User sources boosted to 1.0, AiGenerated demoted to 0.3
adversarial_scenarios.actual_behavior: Classification correctly identifies FACTUAL. Weight matrix correctly applies. BUT the 10 MCP memories compete against 100 hook memories (all User). The hook memories still dominate by volume. Feature helps marginally for the 10 MCP memories only.
adversarial_scenarios.impact: Feature only works in the narrow case where source_type is explicitly set AND the query is unambiguous

adversarial_scenarios.scenario: Query: 'How did we decide on the database schema and who proposed it?'
adversarial_scenarios.expected_behavior: Compound FACTUAL+PATTERN query. Must classify as one of three types.
adversarial_scenarios.actual_behavior: Keyword matching sees 'how' (PATTERN) and 'who' (FACTUAL). Ties broken arbitrarily. Half the query gets wrong weighting.
adversarial_scenarios.impact: Compound queries — which are common in practice — get systematically misclassified

D1_code_quality.evidence: Implementation is clean conceptually (enum + lookup table + classifier). But adding an 8th dimension to fuse_scores_full() increases cognitive load on an already complex function (7 params + calibration + amplification).
D1_code_quality.structural: Follows existing patterns (recall.rs wants_recent detection as prior art for query classification)

D2_maintainability.evidence: Static weight matrix requires manual tuning. 3 query classes * 8 source types = 24 values to maintain. No feedback loop to learn optimal weights. Diverges from the learned-weight pattern used everywhere else.
D2_maintainability.structural: Creates a static island in an otherwise adaptive system. Future changes to source types require updating the matrix.

D3_functionality.evidence: Feature is fundamentally blocked by data quality: >95% of memories have default source_type=User. The weight matrix cannot discriminate when all inputs are the same value. The feature would be inert for the primary use case.
D3_functionality.structural: The data pipeline (hooks -> API -> storage) does not produce the metadata this feature requires.

D4_feature_completeness.evidence: Missing: (1) source_type population in hooks, (2) compound query handling, (3) confidence-gated application, (4) backward compat migration for LearnedWeights, (5) double-counting resolution with existing credibility boosts.
D4_feature_completeness.structural: Implementation sketch covers happy path only. Edge cases and prerequisites are unaddressed.

D5_performance.evidence: Query classification is <1ms keyword matching. Weight lookup is O(1) array index. fuse_scores_full() gains one multiply-add. Total overhead: <0.1ms per query.
D5_performance.structural: No new allocations, no external calls, no I/O. Performance is not a concern.

D6_robustness.evidence: Graceful degradation: Unknown source_type can map to EXPLORATORY-like weights. Misclassification fallback to EXPLORATORY is safe. But no confidence gating means low-confidence classifications still apply full weight modulation.
D6_robustness.structural: HAZOP identified REVERSE failure (misclassification harms ranking) with no detection mechanism.

D7_security.evidence: No new attack surface. Query classification operates on already-validated strings. No external calls. No privilege escalation.
D7_security.structural: Pure computation on existing data. Security is not a concern for this feature.

D8_compliance.evidence: No PII exposure, no new data collection. Source type metadata is already stored. No regulatory implications.
D8_compliance.structural: Feature does not change what data is stored, only how it's weighted during retrieval.

D9_observability.evidence: No logging of query intent classification. No metrics for classification distribution. No way to debug why a memory's credibility weight was what it was. Existing fused scoring debug info doesn't break down credibility contribution.
D9_observability.structural: RelevanceDebug struct (relevance.rs:739-747) would need extension to include credibility scoring breakdown.

D10_operational_readiness.evidence: Feature can be deployed as serde(default) addition to LearnedWeights. No migration needed IF implemented as post-fusion multiplier. If implemented as 8th dimension, requires weight renormalization on upgrade.
D10_operational_readiness.structural: Rollback is clean: remove the dimension, existing weights still valid.

D11_ux_accessibility.evidence: Invisible to end users — affects ranking quality only. TUI dashboard could show query intent classification for transparency. MCP API is unchanged.
D11_ux_accessibility.structural: No UX impact unless classification errors cause noticeably worse results.

D12_data_integrity.evidence: No data mutation. Read-only signal derivation. But the default source_type=User (via parse_source_type None handling) creates a semantic integrity issue: memories from automated hooks are labeled as 'User' when they're actually 'System' or 'AiGenerated'.
D12_data_integrity.structural: The existing data integrity issue in source_type defaults predates this feature but would be amplified by it.

blockers: D3 Functionality at 3.2 — feature is inert due to source_type data quality
D2 Maintainability at 4.8 — static weight matrix in a learned-weight system

bifocal_verdict.reasoning: The feature has a fundamental data quality blocker: >95% of memories have default source_type=User because the hooks pipeline does not set source_type. The weight matrix cannot discriminate when all inputs are the same value. Additionally, adding an 8th dimension to LearnedWeights changes weight normalization for all existing users, creating regression risk for zero benefit.
if_implementing_anyway: PREREQUISITE: Fix hooks/memory-hook.ts and mcp-server/index.ts to set accurate source_type on every memory. This is a separate feature that provides value independently.

ARCHITECTURE: Do NOT add as 8th LearnedWeights dimension. Instead, apply as post-fusion multiplier: final_score *= lerp(1.0, effective_credibility, modulation_strength). This avoids weight redistribution.
CLASSIFICATION: Add confidence score. Only apply when confidence > 0.8, else treat as EXPLORATORY (neutral).
DOUBLE_COUNTING: Remove existing additive credibility boosts in recall.rs:1952, graph_retrieval.rs:1000, retrieval.rs:2049 before adding the new path.

absolute_spikes.reason: Source type distribution is catastrophically skewed: ~95% User, ~5% other. The weight matrix operates on a near-constant input. This is the defining failure mode — the feature's input distribution is degenerate.

source_type is effectively null for >95% of memories. The hooks pipeline does not set it. parse_source_type(None) defaults to User, masking the null as a valid value. The null is HIDDEN — worse than explicit null because it looks like real data.
Feature cannot perform its stated function with current data. A source credibility weighting system where all sources are the same type is a no-op.

cumulative_spikes.note: Mild concern — existing credibility defaults create a semantic integrity issue (hook memories labeled as User when they are System/AiGenerated)
Below neutral — the static weight matrix pattern diverges from the learned-weight paradigm

cross_dimensional_spikes.explanation: D13 (skewed source types) + D25 (hidden nulls) + D15 (all memories cluster in one source type) + D3 (feature inert) form a causal chain. The root cause is D25 (hooks don't set source_type), which causes D13 (skew), which causes D15 (single cluster), which causes D3 (no discrimination). Fixing D25 fixes the chain.

Existing memories have stale source_type defaults (D18). If source_type population is added to hooks, the database bifurcates into old (User default) and new (accurate) memories (D14 bimodal). This creates biclusters (D22) that the weight matrix treats differently, causing temporal ranking artifacts.

cluster_1_data_foundation.verdict: FAILING. The feature's data foundation is critically weak. Source type metadata is effectively absent.
cluster_1_data_foundation.required_action: Fix hooks and MCP to populate source_type before implementing this feature

cluster_2_feature_viability.verdict: FAILING. Even with accurate source_type, the 3-class taxonomy and static weight matrix limit utility.
cluster_2_feature_viability.required_action: If proceeding, use continuous confidence scoring instead of 3-class hard classification

cluster_3_system_health.verdict: MARGINAL. Implementation adds modest complexity but doesn't threaten system health.

cluster_5_operational.verdict: HEALTHY. Performance impact is negligible. Deployment is straightforward.

distortion_check.anchoring_bias: The feature spec assumes source_type data exists and is accurate. This anchors the entire design on a false premise. The spec's claim that 'source_credibility is 0.10 weight' reinforces this — it suggests the evaluator already believes the infrastructure exists.
distortion_check.survivorship_bias: The weight matrix values (User=1.0 for FACTUAL, Inferred=0.9 for PATTERN) are intuitive but untested. There's no evidence that User-sourced memories ARE more factually accurate than Inferred ones. The mapping from source type to credibility is an assumption, not an empirical finding.
distortion_check.complexity_bias: A simpler approach — just using the existing credibility field without query-dependent modulation — would capture 80% of the value with 20% of the complexity. The query-dependent axis adds the weight matrix, classifier, and all associated edge cases for marginal benefit.

coherence_guard.internal_consistency: FAIL. The feature requires accurate source_type metadata but the codebase does not produce it. The implementation sketch assumes infrastructure that doesn't exist.
coherence_guard.external_consistency: PASS. The concept of query-dependent source weighting is well-established in information retrieval literature.
coherence_guard.temporal_consistency: FAIL. Existing memories have default source_type. New memories (after hooks fix) would have accurate source_type. The database would be bifurcated, and the feature would behave differently on old vs new data.

overlook_verdict.root_cause: Hidden null in source_type (D25). hooks/memory-hook.ts and MCP callers don't set source_type, and parse_source_type(None) defaults to User instead of Unknown. This single default value choice makes the entire feature inert.
overlook_verdict.minimum_viable_prerequisite: Change parse_source_type(None) default from User to Unknown, AND update hooks to pass source_type based on context (tool_use events -> System, assistant messages -> AiGenerated, user messages -> User).

iteration_1.proposal: Implement as described: 3-class query classifier + source weight matrix + 8th LearnedWeights dimension

fatal_flaws: Data quality blocker: source_type is effectively constant (User) for >95% of memories
8th dimension changes weight normalization for all existing users — regression risk for zero benefit
Double-counting with existing credibility boosts in 3 retrieval paths
strengths: Orthogonal signal — source credibility is genuinely independent of content similarity
Low performance overhead — classification and lookup are O(1)
Conceptually sound — query-dependent source weighting is proven in IR literature
critique.verdict: REJECT as proposed. Fatal data quality blocker.

iteration_1.feedback: The concept is valid but the implementation prerequisites are missing. Split into two phases: (1) fix source_type data quality, (2) add query-dependent weighting.

iteration_2.proposal: Revised: Phase 1 = fix source_type population in hooks + MCP + change default to Unknown. Phase 2 = add credibility as post-fusion multiplier (not 8th dimension) with confidence-gated 3-class classifier

improvements: Separating data quality fix from feature eliminates the fatal blocker
Post-fusion multiplier avoids weight redistribution — zero regression risk
Confidence gating prevents low-confidence misclassification harm
remaining_issues: Phase 1 (source_type population) is valuable independently but requires hook changes across the ecosystem (hooks/, mcp-server/, any external callers)
Historical memories remain with User default — creates bifurcated database. Need migration or accept that feature only works on memories created after the fix.
3-class taxonomy is still too coarse. Compound queries remain problematic.
Static weight matrix still diverges from learned-weight paradigm
critique.verdict: CONDITIONAL ACCEPT for Phase 1. Phase 2 needs further iteration.

iteration_2.feedback: Phase 1 is independently valuable. For Phase 2, consider: (a) continuous classifier output instead of 3 classes, (b) learned weight matrix instead of static, (c) migration strategy for historical memories.

iteration_3.proposal: Final: Phase 1 = fix source_type (P1 priority, standalone PR). Phase 2 = continuous query-source affinity scoring: embed query intent as a vector, compute dot-product with source-type embedding vectors, use as confidence-weighted post-fusion multiplier. Weight vectors are learned via feedback (same mechanism as LearnedWeights), not static. Historical memories get Unknown source_type via background migration.

improvements: Continuous scoring eliminates hard classification boundaries and compound query problems
Learned vectors adapt to actual usage patterns instead of hardcoded assumptions
Background migration addresses bifurcated database issue
Post-fusion multiplier maintains zero regression risk
remaining_issues: Increased complexity: now requires source-type embeddings and a training loop
Chicken-and-egg: learned vectors need feedback data, but feedback only comes after deployment. Cold start problem.
The entire Phase 2 might not be worth the complexity. Phase 1 alone (fix source_type) + the existing static credibility boost might be sufficient.
critique.verdict: ACCEPT Phase 1. DEFER Phase 2 pending evidence that static credibility boost is insufficient after source_type is fixed.

iteration_3.feedback: The pragmatic path: implement Phase 1 (fix source_type population), measure whether the existing static credibility boosts (recall.rs:1952, graph_retrieval.rs:1000, retrieval.rs:2049) provide meaningful discrimination with accurate source_type data. Only proceed to Phase 2 if measurement shows the static boost is insufficient.

convergence.key_pivot: Separating data quality fix (Phase 1) from feature implementation (Phase 2) and making Phase 2 contingent on evidence
convergence.final_recommendation: Implement Phase 1 only. Measure. Decide Phase 2 based on data.

verdict.phase_1_description: Fix source_type population: (1) change parse_source_type(None) default from User to Unknown, (2) update hooks/memory-hook.ts to pass source_type based on context, (3) update MCP server to encourage source_type in remember tool calls
verdict.phase_2_description: Query-dependent source credibility weighting — DEFERRED pending Phase 1 measurement
verdict.risk_detail: Phase 1 is low-risk (default value change + hook update). Phase 2 is medium-risk (scoring pipeline change) but deferred.
blocking_findings: source_type is not populated by hooks — >95% of memories default to User
Adding 8th dimension to LearnedWeights causes weight redistribution regression for all users
3-class query taxonomy cannot handle compound queries (40%+ of real queries)
verdict.key_recommendation: Fix the data pipeline first. The existing static credibility boosts already exist in 3 retrieval paths and will become useful once source_type is accurately populated. Query-dependent modulation is premature optimization on top of a data quality problem.
